prose: This positive call at the assay_mechanism_recovered tier rests on curated mechanistic convergence: chlorpyrifos carries CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence and is a registered stressor within a neuro-AOP [E1], with an independent curated line flagging it as an established human neurotoxicant [E4]. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis anchors the molecular initiating event in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits, propagating to an adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1-vulnerable dopaminergic population [E2] — a cell-type grounding that lends biological coherence to the pathway. Brain-penetrant structure-based prediction supports plausible CNS exposure [E6], and organophosphate-class cohorts report elevated Parkinson's risk [E5], offering convergent but non-causal contextual support. The 42 ToxCast assay hits [E3] illustrate the mechanistic chain as corroboration only; being anti-diagnostic, they are not treated as decisive. Collectively, the curated mechanism drives the call, while epidemiology, exposure plausibility, and assay signals remain supportive rather than determinative. No causal claim is asserted.

citations.detail: Curated convergence: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence AND registered neuro-AOP stressor.
citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP 3: Inhibition of the mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (WPHA/WNT Endorsed).

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
citations.reference: Rath S, Sharma R, Gupta R, et al. MitoCarta3.0: an updated mitochondrial proteome now with sub-organelle localization and pathway annotations. Nucleic Acids Research. 2021;49(D1):D1541-D1547. Kamath T, Abdulraouf A, Burris SJ, et al. Single-cell genomic profiling of human dopamine neurons identifies a population that selectively degenerates in Parkinson's disease. Nature Neuroscience. 2022;25(5):588-595.

citations.detail: 42 mechanistic assay hits illustrate the chain (corroboration only — anti-diagnostic, not a discriminator).
citations.reference: U.S. EPA ToxCast/Tox21 invitroDB (v4.2), accessed through the NICEATM Integrated Chemical Environment (ICE). Active hitcalls only; queried on the salt-form-correct record.

citations.detail: Independently listed as an established human neurotoxicant (Grandjean & Landrigan 2014, Lancet Neurol) — a curated line independent of CTD and AOP-Wiki.
citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP 3: Inhibition of the mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (WPHA/WNT Endorsed).

PD elevated (OP class) (Costello/Wang OP-PD studies). Quantified PD/AD exposure risk curated from published cohorts and meta-analyses (e.g., Tanner et al. 2011, Environmental Health Perspectives; Pezzoli & Cereda 2013, Neurology).

Meng F, Xi Y, Huang J, Ayers PW. A curated diverse molecular database of blood-brain barrier permeability with chemical descriptors (B3DB). Scientific Data. 2021;8:289. Daina A, Zoete V. A BOILED-Egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain access of small molecules. ChemMedChem. 2016;11(11):1117-1121.
